title: betula fruticosa subsp. extremiorientalis
intro: Betula fruticosa subsp. extremiorientalis, also known as the Far Eastern birch, is a subspecies of the Betula fruticosa plant, which is native to the Russian Far East and northeastern China. This deciduous shrub or small tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects. The health benefits of Betula fruticosa subsp. extremiorientalis include its ability to help reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, or used in tinctures and salves for topical application, making it a valuable resource in natural medicine.
